title: ConformanceDecisionResponse
description: The outcome the conformance runner writes for one ConformanceDecisionRequest (spec ¬ß6.3.1). The runner reads a JSON ARRAY of requests on stdin and writes a JSON ARRAY of these outcomes on stdout ‚Äî one per request, IN THE SAME ORDER ‚Äî then exits 0; the array position is the sole correlation between a request and its response. A decision either ACCEPTS, emitting the events the Decider produced (`emit`), or REJECTS, naming the invariant that blocked it (`reject`). REJECT WINS: if a response object carries BOTH keys, the response is a rejection and `reject` is authoritative ‚Äî a runner that has both an emission and a rejection to report is reporting a rejection. An accept with no resulting events is `{ "emit": [] }`. Every emitted payload value is a wire scalar (¬ß3.3): `boolean`, 64-bit signed `integer`, or `string`; a `number`- or `date`-declared field travels AS A STRING. Self-contained: no cross-file `$ref`; all reusable shapes inline in `$defs`.

emit.description: The events the Decider produced, in emission order, when the command was ACCEPTED. May be empty (`[]`) ‚Äî an accept that produces no event. If `reject` is also present, this field is ignored: reject wins.

reject.description: The id of the invariant that rejected the command. Its presence makes this response a REJECTION regardless of any `emit`: when both keys appear, reject wins.

scalar.description: A wire scalar (¬ß3.3): the only value types that cross this wire. `boolean`, a 64-bit signed `integer`, or a `string`. A payload field declared `number` or `date` (¬ß3.2) is carried here AS A STRING ‚Äî the wire alphabet has no fractional-number or date type, so those cross as their string form.

eventRef.description: A reference to an event with its payload. EITHER a bare id string (shorthand for that event with the EMPTY payload) OR an object naming the `event` id and its `with` payload. A missing `with` is the empty payload. Payload values are wire scalars (¬ß3.3).

oneOf.description: Bare event id ‚Äî the event with an empty payload.

with.description: The event payload: field name ‚Üí wire scalar. Absent means the empty payload.
